blurb: Microsoft’s policy footprint is unusually legible through On the Issues, EU Policy Blog and formal AI-safety testing arrangements with CAISI/AISI. It is also a named defendant in the consolidated publisher copyright litigation alongside OpenAI.

hiring.note: Official Microsoft careers search redirects to JS app; needs browser/API extraction for current London policy/legal roles.

publications.title: Advancing AI evaluation with the Center for AI Standards (US) and Innovation and the AI Security Institute (UK)
publications.summary: Microsoft announces agreements with US CAISI and the UK AI Security Institute to test Microsoft frontier models, assess safeguards and mitigate national-security and large-scale public-safety risks.

publications.title: AI, jobs, and the next generation
publications.summary: Brad Smith sets out Microsoft’s view on AI, labour-market impact, skills and workforce policy — a named-principal contribution to the displacement/augmentation debate.

publications.title: Strengthening biosecurity in the era of AI
publications.summary: Eric Horvitz discusses AI biosecurity safeguards, connecting Microsoft’s safety work to the frontier-lab biosecurity-policy conversation.

publications.title: AI for Europe, in Europe’s own languages
publications.summary: Microsoft EU Policy Blog post on European-language AI access and infrastructure, a regional policy-positioning signal for Microsoft’s European AI work.

publications.title: Powering Europe’s AI future: The fusion of compute and power
publications.summary: Microsoft EU Policy Blog post tying AI expansion to compute and power infrastructure — directly relevant to European AI-capacity policy.

publications.title: Scaling cybercrime disruption through innovation and AI
publications.summary: Microsoft describes an AI-assisted Digital Crimes Unit action against Amadey and StealC infrastructure, including Europol/industry coordination and expanded RICO legal strategy for cybercrime disruption.

publications.title: Working constructively with the UK CMA to support customer choice and cloud competition
publications.summary: Microsoft announces UK Azure changes on data egress, switching and interoperability in response to the CMA Cloud Market Investigation — a directly UK-relevant competition/regulatory move.

publications.title: Reaffirming our commitment to child safety in the face of European Union inaction
publications.summary: Microsoft criticises EU legislative inaction on CSAM detection and restates its voluntary child-safety measures.

publications.title: One year on: Progress on our European digital commitments
publications.summary: Anniversary review of Microsoft’s European Digital Commitments on sovereignty, data and AI governance.

publications.title: From capability to responsibility: Securing our global digital ecosystem with next-generation AI
publications.summary: Security and governance framing for deploying next-generation AI across the digital ecosystem.

publications.title: Strengthening our approach to tackling non-consensual intimate imagery
publications.summary: Digital-safety policy update on detecting and removing non-consensual intimate imagery.

controversies.title: New York Times & news publishers v OpenAI/Microsoft — copyright infringement (SDNY)
controversies.summary: Microsoft is named alongside OpenAI as a defendant in the consolidated New York Times/news-publisher copyright litigation over AI training and outputs. NPR reported in March 2025 that Judge Sidney Stein allowed core copyright claims to proceed while narrowing some claims; the case remained unresolved.

notes: launch_harvest 2026-06-27: Microsoft RSS feeds verified HTTP 200: https://blogs.microsoft.com/on-the-issues/feed/ and https://blogs.microsoft.com/eupolicy/feed/. AISI/CAISI article direct fetch HTTP 200. NPR controversy source direct fetch HTTP 200; no paywalled body reproduced. followup_harvest 2026-06-28: Microsoft On the Issues RSS verified 2026-06-24 cybercrime/AI policy post; AI Policy Daily and FLI newsletter were used only as discovery leads where relevant, with first-party URLs retained. followup_harvest 2026-06-28: added five 30–90 day first-party Microsoft policy/safety/competition posts (incl. UK CMA cloud item) verified via On the Issues / EU Policy indexes.
